problem_summary: Stochastic (Gillespie) SIR model with latent (beta, gamma). Observations are noisy infected counts I(xi_k) at strictly increasing measurement times; BOED picks each measurement time.

latent_spec.notes: beta is the contact rate (1/time), gamma is the recovery rate (1/time). R0 = beta / gamma is truncated to [0.5, 10] in the prior.

observation_spec.notes: Single noisy observation of the infected *fraction* y = I(xi)/N per round. Step-function lookup on one ground-truth Gillespie trajectory, then additive Gaussian noise with sigma = 0.005 in fraction-of-population space (≈0.5% of the population; see examples.cases.sir.simulator.DEFAULT_SIGMA_OBS and sequential_lfiax.TrajectoryCache.observe).

design_variables.name: xi
design_variables.description: Next measurement time, optimised subject to xi > xi_prev via examples.cases.sir.monotone.next_xi_from_raw.

hints.prior_notes: log-normal priors with log(beta) ~ N(log 0.3, 0.5^2), log(gamma) ~ N(log 0.1, 0.5^2), truncated to R0 in [0.5, 10].
hints.notes: This bundle is designed for the sequential BOED driver under examples/agent/sir_sequential_agent.py.

data.observed_data_summary: No fixed dataset; one ground-truth trajectory is sampled per run from simulator.simulate_sir_trajectory(theta_true).

candidates.name: sir_gillespie_lfiax
candidates.description: Stochastic Gillespie SIR with LFIAX-style likelihood-free sequential BOED.

metadata.theta_format: length-2 vector [beta, gamma]; both positive, log-normal prior truncated on R0.

metadata.notes: Defaults mirror examples.cases.sir.simulator (DEFAULT_N=500, DEFAULT_I0=2, DEFAULT_SIGMA_OBS=0.005 in fraction-of-population space); Ivanova et al. (2021) / Zaballa & Hui (2025) setup.
